description: Next-session forecasts for all holdings and market indices. Generated before each trading session. Accuracy is tracked session-by-session to build a feedback loop.

next_session.dominant_variable: Islamabad talks outcome (10:00 AM IST) — US delegation led by VP Vance, Iran delegation led by FM Araghchi. Key red lines exposed: Iran demands Hormuz oversight + US military pullback; US holds firm on nuclear enrichment. CNN: 'teetering ceasefire'. SECONDARY: TCS Q4 results strong (revenue BEAT, TCV record $12B) — positive pre-open tailwind for IT sector.
next_session.tcs_results_impact: TCS Q4 results announced post-market April 9. Revenue ₹70,698 cr (BEAT), PAT ₹13,718 cr, TCV $12B Q4 (highest ever), final dividend ₹31/share. TCS closed at ₹2,587.75 on April 9. Expect TCS gap-up 1-2% to ₹2,620-2,640 on April 10 open. Nifty IT positive pre-open tailwind offset by talks uncertainty.
next_session.islamabad_talks_pre_signal: CAUTIOUS. CNN analysis April 9: 'teetering ceasefire bodes ill for treacherous US-Iran talks ahead'. Key gaps: Iran wants Hormuz oversight + full US military withdrawal; US won't budge on nuclear enrichment red line. Netanyahu separately said ceasefire doesn't cover Lebanon. Probability distribution revised: 20% progress, 45% neutral, 35% fail.

scenarios.label: Talks Progress — DE-ESCALATION_DEEPENING
scenarios.description: Talks show concrete progress: both sides agree on nuclear framework or at minimum extend ceasefire to 4+ weeks. Rare given exposed red lines but possible. TCS beat adds tailwind. REVERSAL TRIGGER applies (FORECAST_PAT003).
scenarios.market_tone: Gap-up open +1.5-2%. TCS/IT leads. Aviation (+3-5%), auto follow. Defense premium compresses (ASTRA MICRO -3-5%). SETFGOLD falls sharply. P019 ADANI PORT target within reach.

scenarios.label: Talks Neutral / Ongoing — DE-ESCALATION_REAL holds
scenarios.description: Talks begin but no breakthrough — sides agree to continue. 2-week ceasefire timeline intact. Market trades on TCS results primarily. Nifty recovers modestly from April 9 dip.
scenarios.market_tone: Flat-to-modest recovery. IT sector (TCS gap-up) leads. Broad market cautiously holds. Nifty recovers 23,800-24,000. Talks continuation = next review April 22 ceasefire expiry.

scenarios.label: Talks Fail — WAR_PREMIUM_RETURN
scenarios.description: Islamabad talks break down on nuclear enrichment red line or Hormuz oversight demand. Ceasefire in doubt. War premium returns sharply.
scenarios.market_tone: Sharp gap-down -2-3%. Aviation sells off (INDIGO -5-7%). Gold spikes (SETFGOLD +3-5%). Defense stocks rally (ASTRA MICRO potentially +5%). FII selling resumes. P016 Nifty range thesis invalidated.

next_session.base_case_scenario: Base (Talks Neutral, 45%) with TCS results providing IT-sector floor. Nifty likely 23,700-24,000 unless talks outcome resolves sharply.
next_session.note: April 10 is the highest-stakes session. Confidence LOW (★★) — 3-way binary. Probability revised from 30/35/35 (morning) to 20/45/35 (EOD) based on exposed red lines in pre-talks analysis. TCS beat is a positive independent factor (+0.3-0.5% sector tailwind). Applying FORECAST_PAT003: REVERSAL TRIGGER if talks succeed = regime flips to +1.5-2% Nifty. REVERSAL TRIGGER if talks fail = regime flips to -2-3% Nifty. Do NOT bias toward single scenario. KEY WATCH: Nifty IT opening gap (TCS reaction) vs broad market reaction to talks outcome — these may diverge for first 30 mins.

previous_sessions.error_analysis: WRONG. Forecast range 23,800-24,100, actual close 23,775.10 — 25 pts BELOW lower bound. Forecast direction FLAT_TO_SLIGHTLY_UP, actual -0.93%. Root cause: Trump's statement that US-Iran situation 'remains tense until a real pact is reached' dampened post-ceasefire optimism more than expected. Bloomberg noted India rally showed 'signs of short covering, lacks fresh buying' — indicating April 8 rally was partially technical. TCS Q4FY26 results (post-market) were strong (revenue beat, record TCV $12B, ₹31 dividend, AI crossing $2.3B) but announced after market hours — positive catalyst deferred to April 10. Running session accuracy: 1 correct / 3 scored = 33.3%.

EBIT margin 25% FY26 full year (+70bps YoY, highest in 4 years). Deal TCV Q4: $12 billion (highest ever). FY26 TCV: $40.7 billion. AI revenue: $2.3B annualized. Mega deals: 3 in Q4, 5 in FY26. Final dividend ₹31/share (total FY26: ₹110/share). OVERALL: BEAT on revenue, strong TCV, dividends above expectations.

First session of FY27. No prior forecast was generated for this session.
Forecast SIDEWAYS_UP (+0.4%). Actual: Nifty +0.68%. Direction CORRECT. IT sector decoupled from geopolitical risk — HCLTECH +2.73%, INFY +2.54%, TCS +2.66%.
previous_sessions.error_analysis: Direction correct. Magnitude slightly underestimated (+0.68% vs +0.4% forecast). IT/USD decoupling was the key driver — now tracked as FORECAST_PAT001.

previous_sessions.notes: Forecast BINARY_VOLATILE (base -0.8%, extension scenario). Actual: Nifty +3.78% on CEASEFIRE DEAL + RBI rate unchanged. Direction WRONG — underweighted deal scenario (assigned 20% probability, it materialised at 100%). Brent crashed to $93.13 (-17.55%). IndiGo hit upper circuit at ₹4,614.90. Best day for Nifty in 11 months.
previous_sessions.error_analysis: Deal scenario materialised — the 20% probability outcome. Lesson (FORECAST_PAT003): For binary geopolitical forecasts, best-case outcomes tend to be underweighted. When deal/ceasefire materialises, regime flips entirely — not incrementally. Future forecasts must include explicit reversal trigger: 'If deal → +2-4% Nifty rally'. Also: RBI rate unchanged on same day amplified the rally — always check central bank calendar on binary event days.
